description_long: This town hall is a large, multi-story building with a prominent brick facade and numerous tall, arched windows on the lower floors, giving it a stately and institutional appearance. The structure features a main central block with a slightly recessed section on one side and a smaller, attached wing, both topped with flat roofs. The roofscape is detailed with various rooftop elements such as HVAC units and skylights, adding to the realism and complexity of the building. The main entrance is centrally located and framed by a series of arches, suggesting a formal and welcoming entryway. The facade is adorned with subtle decorative moldings and cornices, contributing to a classic, early 20th-century civic architectural style. The overall color palette consists of warm brick tones and light gray roofing, with large windows providing visual rhythm and transparency.
description: A stately, multi-story brick town hall with arched windows, decorative moldings, and a prominent central entrance, featuring flat roofs and realistic rooftop details.
description_view_0: The front view highlights the main entrance with arched windows and a symmetrical brick facade, as well as the attached side wing.
description_view_1: This side view shows the building's full length, revealing the repetitive window pattern and the flat, detailed roofscape.
description_view_2: The rear view displays the back facade with fewer decorative elements and a clear view of the attached wing's side.
description_view_3: This opposite side view emphasizes the plainer side of the building and the rear of the attached wing, with minimal ornamentation.
attribute_facade_ornamentation: Arched windows, decorative cornices, subtle moldings, and pilaster-like vertical elements
attribute_entrance_design: One main central entrance with double doors, framed by a series of arches and decorative moldings, no visible steps or portico
